what_it_does: It is a build-prep implementation guide for constructing the 'Live, Guided, Right-Hand-Man Review Surface'. It breaks the work into areas A–I and, for each area, states principles, the runtime sequence of operations, do/don't rules with rationales, file paths and their roles, and what existing behavior must be preserved. It also lists standing principles and cross-cutting verification rules.
observations: Divides implementation into nine areas (A–I), each tied to named criteria such as A1–A4, B1–B4, etc.
Includes a file-path role legend: NEW, MODIFY, REUSE-import, REFERENCE.
Every area ends with a 'Preserves' section naming existing systems that must keep working unchanged.
Standing principles include one-source/no-parallel-systems, registry-is-truth, fail-loud-legible, simple consent, design-system token fidelity, and no green-painting of feel legs.
Area F is explicitly labeled 'THE MAKE-OR-BREAK' and introduces a mockup-vs-live router plus a mockup-scope resolver.
Area I is labeled 'THE UNIFICATION CENTERPIECE' and treats the surface as binding to the existing walkthrough mode row in MODE_REGISTRY rather than creating a new mode.
Provides specific line-number references into runtime/suite.py, runtime/bridge.py, runtime/implement.py, and canvas/app source files.
Uses pseudocode only where logic is not obvious, for example the F1 present_current mockup branch.
Cross-cutting verification requires FUNCTION (real run), FORM (design tokens/render), needs-Tim legs, and an anti-parallel grep guard.
Explicitly drops approve_reach and forbids reintroducing per-comment consent tiers.
standouts: Area F's mockup-aware stop and mockup-vs-live generate router are framed as the make-or-break frontier.
Area I's unification thesis: the surface is the existing walkthrough mode, not a new mode to add.
The one-source / no-parallel-systems law and registry-is-truth principle are treated as architectural invariants.
The standing 'Preserves' sections make backward-compatibility and non-duplication explicit per area.
Simple consent model: one GENERATE approve plus git revert; approve_reach is dropped.
Fail-loud-legible posture is required everywhere, with the 14KB truncation marker given as the existing example.
conventions: Uses a file-path role legend (NEW / MODIFY / REUSE-import / REFERENCE) for every cited file.
Cites specific source line numbers and function names as evidence for reuse claims.
Each area follows a fixed template: Principles · Sequence of operations · Do/Don't · Files · Preserves.
Rationales are given as italicized 'because' clauses tied to named design laws.
Cross-references two companion documents (Completion Criteria.md and GUIDED-REVIEW-SURFACE.md) as the bar and evidence.
Breaks a typical flat task list by making preservation and non-duplication first-class sections.
concerns: Heavy reliance on specific line numbers in files outside this one; those numbers will drift as the code changes.
Some claims reference observed behavior in other files without showing the code, so a reader must trust the cited line ranges.
Area C notes that C3 (up_translate/coa) is 'possibly declared-not-wired', leaving an unresolved verification gap.
Several 'needs-Tim' legs (streamed feel, real voice, walked-through feel) are explicitly not verifiable by the loop and could stall the build.
Area F is described as make-or-break but still leaves the render/screenshot verification step at a high level without concrete implementation detail.
notes: A dense, area-by-area build plan that treats the guided review surface as an extension of existing backend organs and the MODE_REGISTRY, with explicit preservation and anti-parallel rules.
questions: What is the exact schema and current content of the MODE_REGISTRY walkthrough row?
How is focus.selected structured, and what guarantees it carries a mockup:// address safely?
What is the line-number maintenance policy when the cited source files change?
How does the FE's resolveUiTarget and spotlightUiRef currently behave for unregistered or mockup addresses?
What is the precise contract of up_translate/coa and whether C3 is already wired in the FE?
How are mockup HTML files named, versioned, and rendered for screenshot verification?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file presents itself as a per-area implementation guide and delivers principles, runtime sequences, do/don't rules, file roles, and preservation claims.
apparent_themes: Reuse and preservation of existing circuits
Mockup loop as a second, distinct loop sharing the review room
intention_signals: It is trying to turn an existing autonomous code loop into a live, guided, right-hand-man review surface for a commander by binding frontend affordances to already-built backend organs, while refusing to duplicate steppers, intent paths, scoring sources, or mode tables.
novelty: The explicit 'Preserves' per-area sections, the file-path role legend, the registry-as-truth design law, the anti-parallel guard, and the framing of mockup edits as a separate loop that must not route through the live-app scope resolver.
contribution: It contributes a structured, reusable build plan that documents which existing organs to reuse, which files to modify versus create, and which existing behaviors must stay intact; a fused system would keep this as the map of how the guided surface is meant to attach to the existing runtime and canvas.
summary_for_embedding: Implementation guide for building a live guided review surface by binding frontend components to existing backend walkthrough, chat, R2, and dispatch organs across nine build areas, with explicit reuse, preservation, and anti-parallel rules.
intention_for_embedding: To direct the build so the guided review surface emerges from the existing mode registry and organs rather than spawning parallel systems, while preserving live-app behavior and adding mockup-aware guidance and batch generation.
